description: Single source of truth for the branding portion of the Skill-23 AI Workforce interview. The Command Center vendors a copy of this file and runs a sync test that fails when the two diverge. Edit HERE first; the CC copy updates via the sync test / vendoring workflow.

questions.prompt: What is your primary brand color?
questions.help: If you know the exact hex code (like #1E3A8A) enter it. If not, just type the color name — for example "navy", "forest green", or "coral" — and we'll convert it to the right hex automatically.
questions.resolverHint: Call resolveBrandColor() in src/lib/branding.ts when the value is not a valid hex string.

questions.prompt: Do you have a logo? Paste a public link to it (or upload one).
questions.help: We'll use your logo across the Command Center in place of the default, and upload it to your GoHighLevel media library so it's available for your funnels and emails too.

questions.prompt: What feeling do you want your brand to evoke?
questions.help: Think about the emotional impression you want people to carry with them after any interaction with your brand — not a product feature, but a feeling.
questions.interviewGuidance: Drill until specific. 'Professional' is NOT an answer. Push for an emotion or visceral reaction — confident, rebellious, warm, aspirational.

questions.prompt: What feeling do you want your customers to leave with after working with you?
questions.help: Not what they gained functionally — what they FEEL. Empowered? Relieved? Seen? Ready?
questions.interviewGuidance: Drill the feeling separately from any service description. 'Happy' is not specific enough.

questions.prompt: What words would your best customer use to describe you?
questions.help: Imagine your favorite client is telling a friend about you at dinner. What exact words do they use? Aim for 3–5 words or short phrases.
questions.interviewGuidance: These are the voice/brand-tone inputs that shape every agent's communication style. Pull real testimonial language when available.

questions.prompt: How would you describe your brand voice?
questions.help: Is it formal or casual? Inspirational or tactical? Bold or warm? Pick 2–3 adjectives that feel true, then give an example of a sentence that sounds like YOU.
questions.interviewGuidance: Pre-fill from Phase 0 website/LinkedIn research if confidence is high; frame as confirmation. Used to initialize SOUL.md brand-voice section.

questions.prompt: Who is your ideal customer — and why do they come to YOU specifically?
questions.help: Not a general category. Be specific: who they are, what they want, and what makes them choose you over anyone else who does what you do.
questions.interviewGuidance: Drill until specific. 'Small business owners' = score 2/10 (re-drill). 'Black women entrepreneurs in childcare, over 45, already making $250K/year' = score 9/10 (move on). Drill twice before accepting.

questions.prompt: Why do people come to YOU versus anyone else who does what you do?
questions.help: What is the real reason — not the marketing version. The thing you'd tell a friend, not a prospect.
questions.interviewGuidance: If they give a marketing answer, drill: 'That's what you tell prospects. What do you actually believe makes the difference?' Get the authentic version.

postSectionActions: Write resolved brand_primary_color hex to clients.brand_color via the Command Center API.
Write brand_voice to SOUL.md § Brand Voice block.
Write brand_descriptors + brand_evokes to USER.md § Brand Identity section.

syncTest.description: The Command Center vendors a copy at src/lib/interview-questions.branding-questions.json. The CI sync test (scripts/sync-branding-questions-test.ts in the CC repo) imports this canonical file and the vendored copy and fails if they diverge on any question id, prompt, storeOn, or kind field.
